analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 10 finding(s) from 10 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: JTAG DMI write operations can reach the debug module without an effective jtag_key authorization check; only DMI reads appear conditioned on pass_chk.
findings.vulnerability_category: DMI read/write or debug control trigger / DMI-accessible debug module registers and controls / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: External JTAG/TAP requester Operation: DMI read/write or debug control trigger Object: DMI-accessible debug module registers and controls Expected guard: JTAG/DMI operations should be mediated by jtag_key/lifecycle/debug-enable authorization before enabling DMI register access. Observed behavior: dmi_jtag copies jtag_key into pass on reset and sets pass_chk on a DTM_PASS operation. In the Idle state, DTM_READ transitions to Read only when pass_chk==1, but DTM_WRITE transitions to Write without checking pass_chk. The Write state asserts dmi_req_valid. TAP DMIACCESS selection is driven by the JTAG IR without an authorization condition. Path: External JTAG pins -> dmi_jtag_tap DMIACCESS -> dmi_jtag state machine -> dmi_cdc -> dmi_req_valid_o -> dm_top/dm_csrs Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An unauthenticated external JTAG actor can write DMI registers, enabling debug control, halt/resume/reset, command execution, or debug SBA depending on downstream registers.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full reset behavior of pass_chk and integration-specific lifecycle/debug-enable policy were not shown.

findings.summary: DMI writes can assert hart halt/resume and command controls through dm_csrs without evidence of authorization beyond normal DM register state.
findings.vulnerability_category: halt/resume/execute debug command/enter debug mode / haltreq_o, resumereq_o, cmd_valid_o, debug_req_o and core debug entry state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: DMI debug requester Operation: halt/resume/execute debug command/enter debug mode Object: haltreq_o, resumereq_o, cmd_valid_o, debug_req_o and core debug entry state Expected guard: Halting, resuming, and command execution should require authorized debug state plus valid hart selection and availability checks. Observed behavior: dm_csrs selects a hart from hartsel_o and directly maps dmcontrol_q.haltreq/resumereq to haltreq_o/resumereq_o for the selected hart. Command writes assert cmd_valid_o when not busy. csr_regfile enters debug mode when debug_req_i is asserted and an instruction commits. Path: DMI request -> dm_csrs DMControl/Command decode -> haltreq_o/resumereq_o/cmd_valid_o -> dm_top debug_req_o -> csr_regfile debug_req_i/debug_mode_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An unauthorized DMI writer can stop or resume harts and force entry into debug mode, enabling control of CPU execution and inspection/manipulation of machine state.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Complete dmcontrol write decode and dm_top debug_req_o generation were not fully provided; unavailable_i use was visible as an input but not evidenced as an output guard.

findings.summary: Debug SBA CSR writes can initiate system-bus address/data accesses without observed address-range/PMP/firewall restriction.
findings.vulnerability_category: AXI read/write via sbaddress/sbdata / AXI system memory/peripheral address space reachable by dm_sba / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Debug module SBA/DMI requester Operation: AXI read/write via sbaddress/sbdata Object: AXI system memory/peripheral address space reachable by dm_sba Expected guard: Debug SBA should require debug authorization and range/PMP/firewall checks for protected memory/peripherals. Observed behavior: dm_csrs writes SBAddress0 and SBData0 and asserts sbaddress_write_valid_o or sbdata_write_valid_o when sberror is zero. dm_top exposes an AXI master interface for system bus accesses and instantiates dm_sba. Path: DMI write -> dm_csrs SBCS/SBAddress/SBData -> dm_top i_dm_sba -> axi_m_req_o AXI master fabric Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If DMI is unauthorized or insufficiently constrained, debug SBA can read/write memory or peripherals outside CPU privilege/PMP enforcement.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dm_sba implementation and top-level interconnect address filtering were not included; reachable exact address ranges are therefore not closed.

findings.summary: CSR privilege enforcement is undermined by external umode_i/debug-derived privilege forcing and an explicit MEPC privilege-check exception.
findings.vulnerability_category: CSR read/write/configure / Machine/supervisor/debug CSRs including mstatus-like fields, satp, trap vectors, debug CSRs, cache enables, and performance CSRs / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: CPU CSR requester Operation: CSR read/write/configure Object: Machine/supervisor/debug CSRs including mstatus-like fields, satp, trap vectors, debug CSRs, cache enables, and performance CSRs Expected guard: CSR read/write access should be checked against current privilege/mode and CSR access class; user mode must not modify machine/supervisor/debug CSRs. Observed behavior: csr_regfile computes priv_lvl_o as machine mode whenever debug_mode_q or umode_i is asserted. dmi_jtag sets umode_o based on pass_chk and comments it as setting the processor to machine mode. The CSR privilege check also exempts CSR_MEPC from the normal privilege comparison. Path: dmi_jtag pass_chk -> umode_o integration signal -> csr_regfile umode_i -> priv_lvl_o forced M -> CSR read/write decode and state update Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If umode_i is controllable through the debug/JTAG path, CSR instructions can execute with effective machine privilege, allowing modification of protected machine/supervisor/debug CSRs and security-sensitive controls.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Exact top-level wiring from dmi_jtag.umode_o to csr_regfile.umode_i was not provided in the observations.

findings.summary: Translation and memory-policy controls can be affected through CSR writes under the same privilege-forcing path, and no TVM/TSR/TW enforcement was evidenced at the CSR operation sites.
findings.vulnerability_category: change memory access policy/configure translation / satp, ASID, SUM, MXR, TVM, TW, TSR, translation-enable outputs / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: CPU CSR requester Operation: change memory access policy/configure translation Object: satp, ASID, SUM, MXR, TVM, TW, TSR, translation-enable outputs Expected guard: Only permitted privileged modes should change translation and memory-access policy fields; supervisor restrictions such as TVM/TSR/TW should be enforced when applicable. Observed behavior: SATP write accepts MODE_OFF or MODE_SV39 and updates satp_d. Translation outputs directly reflect satp_q and mstatus fields. priv_lvl_o is forced to machine mode when umode_i is asserted, which also disables en_translation_o because it requires priv_lvl_o != M. Path: CSR write -> csr_regfile SATP/mstatus write decode -> satp_q/mstatus_q -> en_translation_o/en_ld_st_translation_o/satp_ppn_o/asid_o/sum_o/mxr_o/tvm_o/tw_o/tsr_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A requester that gains effective M privilege can disable/alter address translation, ASID/PPN, SUM/MXR and related memory-isolation policy, bypassing intended virtual-memory permissions.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full mstatus write case was truncated; complete enforcement of TVM/TW/TSR outside csr_regfile was not available.

findings.summary: Security-sensitive ROM2 key registers, including the source for jtag_key, are writable through a request/write-enable interface without observed lock or authorization guard.
findings.vulnerability_category: MMIO/APB write/read protected register / ROM2 secure_reg contents and jtag_key-derived key register / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: APB/MMIO bus requester Operation: MMIO/APB write/read protected register Object: ROM2 secure_reg contents and jtag_key-derived key register Expected guard: Writes to jtag_key or protected key/config registers should require decode plus privilege/lock/lifecycle authorization and should avoid secret readback exposure. Observed behavior: rom2 initializes secure_reg from constant key memory on reset but later updates secure_reg[index] with wdata_i whenever req_i and we_i are asserted. ariane_peripherals assigns jtag_key from key_reg_out[1][31:0]. Path: APB/MMIO wrapper -> rom2 req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i -> secure_reg -> key_reg_out -> jtag_key output -> dmi_jtag.jtag_key Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Software or any bus master reaching this interface can overwrite debug/AES/access-control key material, potentially setting the JTAG password to a known value or corrupting security policy.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The exact APB address decode and bus-master authorization to rom2 were not fully visible; openpiton integration details were not provided.

findings.summary: rom2 behaves as a writable key/ROM register file rather than read-only ROM.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write boot ROM or key ROM / ROM2 secure_reg contents and boot/security configuration data / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Bus/MMIO requester Operation: read/write boot ROM or key ROM Object: ROM2 secure_reg contents and boot/security configuration data Expected guard: ROM contents should be read-only to untrusted requesters; write-enable should be ignored or restricted and reads should be range bounded. Observed behavior: When req_i is asserted and we_i is high, rom2 writes wdata_i into secure_reg at an address-derived index. rdata_o reads secure_reg[raddr_q] if raddr_q<RomSize; otherwise zero. Path: rom2 req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i -> secure_reg array -> rdata_o/secure_reg outputs Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An allowed bus write can alter immutable boot/security key material, defeating assumptions that ROM/fuse data cannot be modified after reset.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Interconnect reachability from debug/SBA, NoC, or APB to rom2 write port was not completely closed.

findings.summary: dmi_jtag_tap uses testmode_i only as the select on a clock mux choosing between inverted TCK and direct TCK for testing. testmode_i is present as an input on serpent_peripherals, dmi_jtag, dmi_jtag_tap, dm_top, and dm_csrs in the fact layer.
findings.vulnerability_category: test override/clock route/reset / TAP clocking and DMI/debug path / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Test mode input/interface Operation: test override/clock route/reset Object: TAP clocking and DMI/debug path Expected guard: Test mode should not enable unauthorized debug access or bypass authentication in production unless lifecycle/test-authorized. Observed behavior: dmi_jtag_tap uses testmode_i only as the select on a clock mux choosing between inverted TCK and direct TCK for testing. testmode_i is present as an input on serpent_peripherals, dmi_jtag, dmi_jtag_tap, dm_top, and dm_csrs in the fact layer. Path: testmode_i/test_en -> dmi_jtag_tap pulp_clock_mux2 -> TDO/TAP timing; testmode_i also propagates into debug modules/FIFOs Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need top-level tieoff/source of testmode_i/test_en and any lifecycle/test authorization. No concrete security impact was evidenced from the observed clock mux alone. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-RTL-008.

findings.summary: DMI-accessible dmcontrol can assert ndmreset and dmactive without observed authorization, allowing debug-originated system reset/control.
findings.vulnerability_category: assert reset/activate-deactivate debug module / ndmreset_o, dmactive_o and security-relevant state across reset/debug transitions / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Debug module requester Operation: assert reset/activate-deactivate debug module Object: ndmreset_o, dmactive_o and security-relevant state across reset/debug transitions Expected guard: ndmreset and dmactive should be assertable only by authorized debug requests and should follow reset sequencing policy. Observed behavior: dmactive_o is assigned from dmcontrol_q.dmactive and ndmreset_o from dmcontrol_q.ndmreset. dmcontrol_q fields are reset/cleared when dmactive is deasserted, but no authorization guard is shown on DMI writes reaching dmcontrol. Path: JTAG/DMI write -> dm_csrs DMControl -> dmcontrol_q.dmactive/ndmreset -> dm_top/SoC reset wiring Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An unauthorized debug requester can reset non-debug system components or manipulate debug active state, causing denial of service or clearing/preserving state outside intended reset policy.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Complete DMControl write decode and top-level reset fanout were not fully shown.

findings.summary: fpga/src/ariane_xilinx.sv exposes PCIe pins and instantiates xdma_0 plus AXI data-width and clock converters. The fact layer identifies XDMA and converter instances but does not include interconnect decode/filter logic.
findings.vulnerability_category: external DMA AXI read/write / DDR/system memory and memory-mapped peripherals reachable by XDMA AXI / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: External PCIe XDMA master Operation: external DMA AXI read/write Object: DDR/system memory and memory-mapped peripherals reachable by XDMA AXI Expected guard: External PCIe/DMA masters should be constrained by address range/firewall/IOMMU/PMP-equivalent checks. Observed behavior: fpga/src/ariane_xilinx.sv exposes PCIe pins and instantiates xdma_0 plus AXI data-width and clock converters. The fact layer identifies XDMA and converter instances but does not include interconnect decode/filter logic. Path: PCIe pins -> i_xdma -> i_axi_dwidth_converter_256_64 -> pcie_axi_clock_converter -> AXI fabric/DDR/peripherals Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Security impact is not fully established from visible evidence.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: No address map, firewall/IOMMU, AXI interconnect decode, or blocking behavior evidence was provided; security impact cannot be asserted from instances alone. Missing evidence slots: impact.
recommended_follow_up: Check security-sensitive consequence of the observed behavior for obligation PERM-RTL-010.

global_uncertainty: 2 evidence slot(s) remained incomplete after closure checking.
